title: The three pillars of observability
prompt_md:
Observability — your ability to understand what a running service is doing from the outside — is commonly described as resting on three pillars.

What are the three pillars?
explanation_md: The three pillars are **logs** (timestamped records of discrete events), **metrics** (numeric measurements aggregated over time, like error rate and latency), and **traces** (the timed path of a single request across services). Together they let you debug production without attaching a debugger.
